entries.description: Operator opened Set 048 STUB spec and requested next session. Confirmed prerequisites: Set 047 CLOSED 2026-05-26 (state file v4, status complete, 6 of 6 sessions). Operator answers via AskUserQuestion: (1) NTE budget $10 for Set 048 (matches Set 047 scale; audit-then-spec with 4-6 sessions room for routed verification rounds), (2) Absorb-into-audit-then-run-consensus pattern (operator's four new directives L1-L4 to be folded into spec.md BEFORE the routed audit pass runs). Set 048 spec was a stub authored at Set 047 S1 close-out per the audit-then-spec discipline.

Absorbed four operator-locked directives into docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity/spec.md: (L1) Copyable prompts MUST reference file paths from repo root, NOT embed file contents (revises B2). Rationale: works with any path-aware IDE agent; dodges clipboard-size limits; avoids stale-paste drift. (L2) Hierarchical right-click context menu with Open File and Copy Eval submenus. (L3) Remove 'Open AI Assignment' from right-click context menu. (L4) Context-menu popup must dismiss on focus-loss / Escape and/or expose explicit close-button. Also added new deliverable B7 (context-menu IA refresh per L2+L3+L4); added open audit topics 9 (cursor-anchored popup vs showQuickPick vs hybrid rendering) and 10 (review-criteria customization storage); bumped estimated session arc from 4-6 to 5-7; added Session Set Configuration placeholder with totalSessions=1 (audit-only S1) + prerequisites + tier=full + uatStyle=ad-hoc + effort=high.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity --session-number 1 --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort max --total-sessions 1. State-file flipped from not-started (schemaVersion=3, sessions[]=empty) to in-progress with the v4-canonical writer per Set 047 S4 (schemaVersion=4, sessions[1]={number:1, title:'Session 1', status:in-progress, startedAt, orchestrator block populated}). Confirms Set 047 S4 writer-flip works against a v3 stub correctly.

Authored docs/proposals/2026-05-26-set-048-lightweight-tier-parity/proposal.md (~414 lines, 29879 chars): §1 purpose, §2 carry-forward locked items (P1-P4 + D1-D5 from Set 047 + new L1-L4 from this stub), §3 code-state grounding (writer call sites post-Set-047, install footprint, current context-menu surface, spec.md schema today, review-criteria storage today=nowhere, Marketplace count=3), §4 Group A --no-router mode dispositions (A1 activation precedence CLI>env>spec>default, A2 lazy LLM-SDK imports, A3 verification short-circuit, A4 CLI back-compat), §5 Group B copyable-prompt dispositions (B1 path-reference format per L1, B2 dual-surface enablement rules, B3 clipboard mechanism), §6 Group C context-menu IA refresh (C1 cursor-anchor preservation with HTML submenu was drafter's preference, C2 close-on-blur + close-button belt-and-suspenders, C3 submenu item layout, C4 removed Open AI Assignment), §7 Group D doc-revision pass (D1-D4), §8 Group E open-question dispositions (E1-E10), §9 6-session arc proposed, §10 8 bias-cautions, §11 5 open questions, §12 13 memory-hook citations. Self-authored per feedback_ai_router_usage; cross-provider verification routed at audit's end-of-session boundary.

Ran Pass A of two-pass devil's-advocate consensus via docs/proposals/2026-05-26-set-048-lightweight-tier-parity/run_consensus.py. Primary read (route, gemini-2.5-pro tier 2): ENDORSE WITH REVISIONS. Group A (--no-router) sound; Group B (copyable prompts) UNSOUND — wants content-embed fallback (later flagged as L1 violation by verifier); Group C UNSOUND — wants showQuickPick over HTML submenu; Group D sound (warning-only); session breakdown sound (do not merge S3+S4, do not split publish); 'missing topics' surfaced as telemetry + prompt-template VS Code settings (verifier flagged speculative). Cross-provider verify (gpt-5-4-mini): ISSUES FOUND — (1) Critical: Pass A's content-embed fallback recommendation violates locked premise L1, (2) Major: missing-audit-topic completeness — should have surfaced agent-capability + submenu-accessibility + tri-state-generality as explicit findings, (3) Minor: telemetry/template-settings additions speculative. Pass A cost: $0.0506 ($0.0239 route + $0.0268 verify).

Ran Pass B (devil's-advocate framing) of consensus. Primary read (gemini-2.5-pro): ENDORSE WITH SPECIFIC BIAS FLIPS — FLIPPED Bias 2 partial (wanted content-embed fallback — later flagged as L1 violation by verifier; stand-by adopted), FLIPPED Bias 3 (HTML submenu → showQuickPick — accessibility + keyboard nav + theme respect + forward-compat all free; cursor-anchor regression acceptable), STAND BY Bias 4 (triple-redundancy reminders cost is low), FLIPPED Bias 5 (warning-only → soft gate with interactive [y/N] confirm), STAND BY Bias 6 (repo-level review criteria), FLIPPED Bias 7 (combine S3+S4 — separating creates throwaway work), FLIPPED Bias 8 (ship Set 047 publishes before Set 048 starts — cleaner engineering vs bundled release). Cross-provider verify (gpt-5-4-mini): ISSUES FOUND — Major: Bias 2 inversion recommends content-embed fallback that violates L1 lock. Verifier called out: 'Evaluate Specification (with content)' as a fourth submenu item directly conflicts with locked L1. Pass B cost: $0.0520 ($0.0309 route + $0.0211 verify).

Synthesized 4 consensus reads into docs/proposals/2026-05-26-set-048-lightweight-tier-parity/verdict.md. Locked flips: Bias 3 (QuickPick wins; L4 becomes a free byproduct since QuickPick handles dismiss natively), Bias 5 PARTIAL (soft gate with interactive [y/N] in TTY mode + non-interactive bypass + --accept-suggestions CLI flag override). Locked stand-by: Bias 1 (three-knob activation), Bias 2 (path-reference ONLY — verifier ruled both Pass A and Pass B content-embed recommendations as L1 violations; resolved via UX docs not prompt-format compromise), Bias 4 (triple-redundancy), Bias 6 (repo-level criteria). Open-question resolutions: Q1 CLI>env>spec with log.info notification, Q2 free-form external-verification.md, Q3 tri-state both tiers, Q5 do not add Evaluate Implementation Plan menu item. Bias 7 and Bias 8 surfaced for operator decision (split votes between Pass A endorsing proposal and Pass B inverting). Cumulative S1 routed cost: $0.1027 of $10 NTE (1.0%).

Operator via AskUserQuestion: Bias 7 → COMBINE S3 (copyable-prompt commands) + S4 (context-menu IA refresh) into one session per Pass B's throwaway-work argument. Bias 8 → SHIP Set 047's HELD publishes NOW (separately, before Set 048 S2 starts) per Pass B's stable-baseline argument. Net effect on session arc: 5 sessions (down from proposed 6). Set 047 PyPI 0.9.0 + Marketplace 0.22.0 will publish before S2.

Operator override on Bias 4 stand-by: triple-redundancy reminders (toast + activity-log + close-out) REPLACED by single upfront positive-confirmation prompt issued by the AI orchestrator at session start. When session has UX scope AND requiresUAT/E2E is 'suggested', AI orchestrator asks operator: 'E2E tests, UAT checklist, both, or neither?' Operator's choice recorded once in activity-log.json (kind: suggestion_disposition). No further nag during session. Close-out reads recorded disposition and gates accordingly (neither → proceed, E2E only → require E2E pass evidence, UAT only → require UAT checklist evidence, both → both Full-tier gates apply). Verdict.md updated to reflect override. Set 048 session 3 implementation will own the AI-orchestrator-question wiring; workflow doc Step 1 documents the branch.

Rewrote docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity/spec.md from STUB AUDIT-PENDING to AUDIT-LOCKED. Session Set Configuration: totalSessions=5, prerequisites=[047-state-file-schema-v4-audit], requiresUAT=true, requiresE2E=false, uatStyle=ad-hoc, effort=high, tier=full. §1 what this set ships, §2 operator-locked premises P1-P4 + decisions D1-D5 + new additions L1-L4, §3 scope-locked decisions (3.1 --no-router mode, 3.2 copyable-prompt commands with path-reference format and agent-capability documentation, 3.3 context-menu IA refresh on QuickPick with two-step submenu flow + Open AI Assignment removed + L4 free via QuickPick native dismiss, 3.4 tri-state UAT/E2E runtime with operator-override upfront positive-confirmation prompt replacing triple-redundancy, 3.5 external-verification.md soft gate with TTY/non-TTY branching + --accept-suggestions flag, 3.6 spec.md schema additions tier+tri-state, 3.7 per-consumer migrator CLI, 3.8 external-verification command, 3.9 review-criteria repo files), §4 5-session breakdown (S1 audit closed, S2 --no-router mode + tri-state schema/runtime + soft gate, S3 combined copyable-prompt + context-menu IA, S4 doc revision + migrator + bootstrap tier-branch, S5 UAT + change-log + version bumps + publish), §5 non-goals (v4 schema = 047, package split = 047 Bias 1 flip, auto-verification excluded by P3, orchestrator block simplification = 049, cursor-anchor preservation = 048 Bias 3 flip, triple-redundancy = operator override, content-embed fallback = L1 lock), §6 deferred follow-ups Q5 + telemetry, §7 cross-references.

Operator requested S2 start despite Marketplace 0.22.0 publish still queued. Discussed strict Bias-8-ordering vs pragmatic-start with operator via AskUserQuestion. Operator chose Start now: PyPI 0.9.0 already live (the dependency S2 actually needs); Marketplace publish is for end-user delivery and not blocking S2 implementation. Marketplace publish background-polled (task bn2l9l1i4) until completion; failed 3m18s into the Publish-to-VS-Code-Marketplace step with /_apis/gallery timeouts (third Marketplace failure today; same as 3 prior local vsce attempts). Operator directive: 'We will have to publish the extension later.' Saved memory at project_marketplace_0_22_0_queued_unshipped.md.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session --session-number 2 --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort max. Writer flipped sessions[2].status from not-started to in-progress; orchestrator block populated; cumulative routed S1 spend $0.1027 of $10 NTE (1.0%) carried forward per memory feedback_budget_question_scope (no re-ask at session start).

Delegated to Explore subagent: (1) spec.md schema validator surfaces - TS-side at types.ts:47 (SessionSetConfig interface, today: requiresUAT/E2E as booleans, no tier) + fileSystem.ts:187 (parseSessionSetConfig). Python-side has NO existing tier/UAT/E2E parser. (2) LLM-SDK imports: NONE at module level - providers.py uses httpx directly. Lazy-import deliverable (A2) is a NO-OP for this codebase. (3) route() @ __init__.py:221, verify() @ :520 - both module-level functions. (4) mark_session_complete() @ session_state.py:1371 - does NOT call verification directly, calls run_gate_checks. (5) close_session: _build_parser @ :271, verification gate around :1398-1426. (6) CLI argparse: per-module, not shared. (7) Activity-log reader: _read_activity_log @ gate_checks.py:443. (8) spec.md reader: parseSessionSetConfig TS-side only.

Commit A (44a1d45): spec.md schema additions per §3.6. TS: new TriStateFlag = boolean|'suggested' + SessionSetTier = 'full'|'lightweight' type aliases in types.ts. SessionSetConfig interface gains tier field; requiresUAT/E2E widen to TriStateFlag. parseSessionSetConfig in fileSystem.ts updated: defaults to tier='full', accepts 'suggested' (quoted/unquoted), tolerates inline YAML comments. 6 test-fixture files updated for the new required tier field (actionRegistry, flagDecisionForReview, forceClosedBadge, inProgressSetsService, prerequisites, sessionSetsProvider). Python: new ai_router/spec_config.py mirroring TS parser - SessionSetConfig frozen dataclass, parse_session_set_config(), Full-tier-conservative defaults. 16 new unit tests. 10 new TS tests for Set 048 schema additions. tsc --noEmit clean.

Commit B (90b7c0c): --no-router activation infrastructure per §3.1 A1+A2. New ai_router/runtime_mode.py with three-knob precedence resolver (CLI flag > env var DABBLER_NO_ROUTER > spec.md tier field > default). resolve_no_router_mode(cli_flag, session_set_dir) caches via module-level _NO_ROUTER_MODE; is_no_router_mode() returns cached or lazy-resolves from env var. Override logging: log.info when CLI/env contradicts spec tier=full. CLI wiring: --no-router flag added to start_session and close_session argparse + main() resolves runtime_mode at entry-point start. --accept-suggestions added to close_session for non-interactive soft-gate bypass per §3.5. Lazy LLM-SDK imports per §3.1 A2: documented as NO-OP (providers.py already uses httpx). 29 new unit tests covering all precedence paths + override-logging assertions. 0 regressions.

Commit C (1eed29a): route()/verify() short-circuit + external-verification.md soft gate per §3.1 A3 + §3.5. ai_router/__init__.py: new _NO_ROUTER_MODEL constant and _build_no_router_route_stub() / _build_no_router_verification_stub() helpers. route() and verify() prologues short-circuit via runtime_mode.is_no_router_mode() and return zero-cost stubs WITHOUT calling _init() (no config load, no credentials needed). close_session.py three changes: (a) manual_attestation block under args.no_router auto-supplies stock attestation when no --reason-file; (b) method resolution gains args.no_router branch setting method='manual'; (c) NEW soft gate after gates pass + before state flip - fires only under --no-router, reads external-verification.md, branches on --accept-suggestions/TTY/non-TTY, aborts with result='aborted_at_soft_gate' + closeout_failed event on TTY non-affirmative answer. 5 route/verify short-circuit tests + 12 close_session integration tests. 947 Python pass + 0 regressions.

Commit D (bd94205): suggestion_disposition helpers + CLI backcompat tests + deferral note. New ai_router/suggestion_disposition.py with record_suggestion_disposition(set_dir, session_number, choice, *, step_number=None) writer + read_suggestion_disposition_for_session(set_dir, session_number) reader. Entry shape: kind='suggestion_disposition' + choice in {e2e, uat, both, neither}. Writer auto-infers stepNumber, raises on unknown choice, raises on missing activity-log. Reader returns last matching choice for session_number, returns None on missing/malformed, never raises. 20 unit tests. 13 CLI backcompat regression tests verifying pre-Set-048 start_session + close_session invocations still parse and validate unchanged. DEFERRAL NOTE in module docstring + commit message: the close-out gate that USES these helpers is deferred to S3 because Full-tier close_session has NO existing UAT/E2E gate today; adding one would touch Full behavior outside the audit scope. S3 owns the AI-orchestrator-question wiring + workflow doc update + the gate that consumes the recorded disposition.

Ran docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity/run_s2_verification.py. Route (sonnet, tier 2, $0.132) returned ISSUES_FOUND with 7 findings: Critical #1 (route/verify silent-swallow falls back to live LLM under exception); Major #2 (bare imports - false positive, this is the package convention via conftest); Major #3 (race condition on activity-log read-modify-write); Major #4 (resolve_no_router_mode re-entry silently overwrites cache); Important #5 (false-positive tier detection - parser falls back to full-file scan when no canonical block, free-form prose 'tier: lightweight' could silently activate Lightweight mode); Minor #6 (timestamp .astimezone() produces local time, inconsistent with UTC log entries); Suggestion #7 (lazy is_no_router_mode caching). Verify (gemini-pro, $0.015) returned VERIFIED for review quality - confirmed all 7 findings as valid with appropriate severity assessments. S2 verification cost: $0.147 of $10 NTE; cumulative Set 048 spend $0.250 (2.5%).

All 5 actionable Round-A findings addressed in-flight per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope: (C1) route()/verify() prologues now fail-CLOSED - top-level import of runtime_mode + direct call; if is_no_router_mode raises, it propagates rather than silently promoting to a live LLM call; (M4) resolve_no_router_mode no-ops on re-entry, returns cached value with debug log; (M3) record_suggestion_disposition uses write-temp + atomic-rename pattern eliminating the read-modify-write race; (I5) parse_session_set_config reads tier ONLY from inside the canonical YAML block (no full-file fallback), preserves Set 015 plain-text fallback for requiresUAT/E2E/uatScope; (Minor 6) timestamp emits UTC directly via datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(). M2 (bare imports) acknowledged as false-positive (matches existing package convention via conftest.py sys.path manipulation); S7 (lazy-cache consistency) deferred as low-priority documentation matter. 2 new regression tests for I5 fix (test_tier_from_free_form_prose_is_ignored + test_requiresUAT_in_plain_text_still_parses_set015_compat). 982 Python pass + 0 regressions after fixes.

Operator (in a new Claude Opus Max chat) asked for the next session. Discovered session-state.json + session-events.jsonl had uncommitted edits flipping S3 to in-progress under a Codex/gpt-5.4/medium orchestrator block ~20 minutes earlier with NO implementation commits to follow. Asked the operator via AskUserQuestion how to proceed; operator chose 'Take over as Claude Opus' (preserve original startedAt timestamp from the Codex start, swap orchestrator block to claude-code/anthropic/claude-opus-4-7/max, append work_resumed event to ledger). Set 033/036 hard-coordination enforcement is disabled by default per project_set_033_enforcement_disabled.md so no state-writer refusal fired; the takeover is purely an audit-log update.

Surveyed the existing right-click context-menu surface: ActionRegistry.ts (15 actions, flat list), CustomSessionSetsView.showContextMenu (computed applicableActions and posted renderContextMenu to webview), media/session-sets-tree/client.js (~100 lines of cursor-anchored popup state + DOM management), media/session-sets-tree/tree.css (.context-menu* rules), openFile.ts (registers openAiAssignment among other openFile commands), copyCommand.ts (existing copyStartCommand.default + .parallel + copySlug). Confirmed Open File submenu locked to exactly 4 entries by L2: Spec / Activity Log / Change Log / Session State (no UAT Checklist / Reveal Playwright Tests / Reveal Folder in the §3.3 spec table); decided to leave those commands palette-accessible. Confirmed Migrate to v3 schema stays as a Flat action alongside v4 (mutually-exclusive predicate guarantees only one per row); spec listed v4 as illustrative.

Created src/commands/copyPromptCommands.ts (~200 lines) with four prompt builders + the BuildContext dependency-injection seam (readReviewCriteria + fileExists) for testability. Path-reference format per L1: prompts list relative-to-root paths (forward-slash normalized) and NEVER embed session-set artifacts. §3.9 carve-out documented in the file header: review-criteria/<kind>.md is the operator-authored meta-instructions slot, intentionally embedded as inline reviewer checklist rather than referenced. Each builder has its own enabled-when predicate fixed via the ActionRegistry: spec-review always; session-accomplishments when sessionsCompleted > 0; set-accomplishments when state === complete; start-next-session non-terminal-only. copyToClipboard wraps writeText in try/catch with showWarningMessage on failure. All four commands registered as dabbler.copySpecReviewPrompt / dabbler.copySessionAccomplishmentsPrompt / dabbler.copySetAccomplishmentsPrompt / dabbler.copyStartNextSessionPrompt.

Reshaped src/providers/ActionRegistry.ts: added ActionCategory discriminator type ('openFile' | 'copyEval' | 'flat') + categorizedActions(set, supports) helper that partitions applicableActions by category and pre-sorts within each. ROW_ACTIONS rewritten to 14 entries: 4 openFile (Spec/ActivityLog/ChangeLog/SessionState per L2 lock), 4 copyEval (the new dabbler.copy*Prompt commands with gating predicates from §3.2 table), 6 flat (Set Orchestrator…/Open Orchestrator Writer Log + the two migrate predicates + Cancel/Restore). Old entries removed: dabblerSessionSets.openAiAssignment (L3), dabblerSessionSets.openUatChecklist + dabblerSessionSets.revealPlaywrightTests + dabblerSessionSets.openFolder (L2 narrowed Open File to exactly 4), dabblerSessionSets.copyStartCommand.default/.parallel + dabblerSessionSets.copySlug (replaced by §3.2 copy-prompts; pre-existing commands remain palette-accessible). Set Orchestrator… gated to in-progress rows via dedicated when predicate.

Rewrote CustomSessionSetsView.showContextMenu as a two-step vscode.window.showQuickPick flow. The host now computes categorizedActions(set, supports), opens a top-level QuickPick with 'Open File ▸' / 'Copy Eval ▸' chips (when their categories are non-empty) plus flat actions inline; an item selection opens a second-level QuickPick for the chosen submenu. Native QuickPick handles click-outside / Escape / focus-loss (L4 close-on-blur free byproduct). Extracted the pure decision logic to src/providers/rowMenuHelpers.ts (buildTopLevelItems + buildSubmenuItems + planLeftClickActivation) for unit-testability. handleActivateRow wires L5 dual-action: ALWAYS opens spec.md, ALSO copies 'Start the next session of <slug>.' + info toast on non-terminal rows. Deleted the cursor-anchored popup: ~100 lines from media/session-sets-tree/client.js (showCursorContextMenu / ensureContextMenuEl / hideContextMenu / bandForCommandId + click/keydown/resize/scroll handlers + lastContextMenuPos state); the .context-menu* CSS in tree.css; the RenderContextMenuMsg / ContextMenuItem / ExecuteRowCommandMsg from the webview protocol. COMMAND_ALLOWLIST collapsed from 14 entries to just dabblerSessionSets.openSpec (the L5 left-click path is the only webview→host dispatch left).

Rewrote src/test/suite/actionRegistry.test.ts to assert the 14-entry registry, the L2-locked 4-item Open File submenu, the four copyEval gating predicates, the L3 openAiAssignment absence invariant, and the categorizedActions partition. Added src/test/suite/copyPromptCommands.test.ts (~14 tests) covering path-reference format / forward-slash normalization / review-criteria default vs embedded / session-accomplishments change-log conditional / git-command embed / start-next-session payload / sanitizeSlugForPrompt. Added src/test/suite/rowMenuHelpers.test.ts (~16 tests) covering buildTopLevelItems / buildSubmenuItems / planLeftClickActivation pure functions including the fail-CLOSED behavior on unknown state values. Added src/test/playwright/context-menu-quickpick.spec.ts (~2 scenarios) pinning the negative invariant that .context-menu* DOM never appears and the L3 openAiAssignment data-command attribute is absent. Bumped src/test/suite/watcherInventory.test.ts pinned line 148 → 149 (new import shifted extension.ts by one line). Removed all entries for openAiAssignment from package.json + COMMAND_ALLOWLIST + openFile.ts registration.

Ran docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity/run_s3_verification.py. Route (sonnet, tier 2, $0.147) returned ISSUES_FOUND with 8 findings: Critical #1 (L1-compliance: review-criteria content embedded into prompt body — operator-authored carve-out per §3.9, documentation clarified); Critical #2 (potential .kind vs .dabblerKind dispatch-key mismatch — false positive on inspection: actual code reads picked.dabblerKind correctly; comment clarified); Important #3 (backtick-injection in L5 clipboard payload if slug contains backtick — defense-in-depth sanitize applied); Important #4 (unknown future state values fall through to clipboard write — flipped to positive in-progress|not-started check to fail CLOSED); Minor #5 (redundant .slice() in applicableActions — removed); Minor #6 (copyToClipboard swallows no error — wrapped in try/catch with showWarningMessage); Minor #7 (set-accomplishments omits activity-log — added comment documenting spec §3.2's intentional set-vs-session evidence-source distinction); Minor #8 (COMMAND_ALLOWLIST narrowed purpose undocumented — expanded comment). Verify (gemini-pro, $0.014) returned VERIFIED for review quality with explicit confirmation that all findings are accurate and proposed fixes correct. S3 verification cost: $0.161 of $10 NTE; cumulative Set 048 spend $0.411 (4.1%).

All 6 actionable Round-A findings addressed in-flight per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope: (#1 documentation) tightened header comment in copyPromptCommands.ts explaining the §3.9 review-criteria carve-out is intentional (operator-authored meta-instructions, not artifact content); (#2 documentation) expanded interface comment on TopLevelPickItem.dabblerKind explaining why VS Code's QuickPickItem.kind collision matters; (#3 sanitization) new sanitizeSlugForPrompt helper replaces backticks with single-quotes; applied in both buildStartNextSessionPrompt and planLeftClickActivation; (#4 fail-CLOSED) planLeftClickActivation flipped from negative 'state !== complete && state !== cancelled' to positive 'state === in-progress || state === not-started' check so unknown future states skip the clipboard shortcut; (#5 cleanup) removed redundant .slice() in applicableActions; (#6 error handling) copyToClipboard wraps writeText in try/catch with showWarningMessage on rejection; (#7 comment) added intentional-omission comment in buildSetAccomplishmentsPrompt referencing spec §3.2 set-vs-session evidence distinction; (#8 comment) expanded COMMAND_ALLOWLIST comment explaining the narrowed purpose and that any new webview→host dispatch path must add allowed ids explicitly. 3 new regression tests (sanitize-backticks for both prompt + clipboard paths + unknown-state fail-CLOSED). 665 TS pass + 0 net regressions; 2 pre-existing failures unchanged from S2 close-out.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session --session-set-dir docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity --session-number 4 --engine claude-code --provider anthropic --model claude-opus-4-7 --effort max. Writer flipped sessions[3].status from not-started to in-progress; orchestrator block populated; cumulative routed S3-close spend $0.411 of $10 NTE (4.1%) carried forward per memory feedback_budget_question_scope.

Commit A (5fbfbb9): per-consumer Lightweight migrator CLI per §3.7. New ai_router/migrate_lightweight_to_canonical_v4.py (~500 LOC) handles three non-canonical Lightweight shapes via _normalize_to_v3_intermediate (sessionLog[] -> sessions[] rename from great-psalms-scroll-font; per-session + top-level status alias canonicalization; missing-schemaVersion stamping). Routes through existing progress.normalize_to_v4_shape shim. Idempotent (canonical v4 input skips). Refuses pre-v3 (pointer to v2->v3 migrator) and future-schema. Apply mode writes session-state.lwbak.json (distinct from .v3.bak.json so operator can tell which migrator ran) before the new file; one-cycle rollback = one rename. 16 unit tests at ai_router/tests/test_migrate_lightweight_to_canonical_v4.py covering all four shape recognizers, idempotent skip, refusal cases, backup contract, and CLI/JSON output. 998 Python pass, 0 regressions.

Commit B (53aa8cb): dabbler.openExternalVerificationDoc command per §3.8. New tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/externalVerification.ts (~80 LOC). Command Palette only; opens or creates <set>/external-verification.md in an editor tab. Free-form per §3.8 (no templated header). EEXIST treated as benign race; falls through to open. Single-set workspaces skip the picker; multi-set workspaces show a QuickPick. Registered in extension.ts via safeRegister. New command entry in package.json. Watcher-inventory pinned line bumped 149 -> 150 (new import shifted createFileSystemWatcher line). tsc clean; 665 TS pass + 2 pre-existing failures unchanged.

Commit C (1ae5d80): review-criteria template files per §3.9. Three files at docs/review-criteria/{spec,session,set}.md with comment-headers explaining how to edit and delete-to-default semantics. Sample bullets cover repo-specific concerns: audit-lock discipline, scope realism, in-flight Round-A fixes, version-bump correctness, cross-repo notices, cumulative budget reporting, memory carry-forward. Picked up automatically by S3's copyPromptCommands default readReviewCriteria reader (no code change required).

Commit D (50db03e): wizard tier-branch (E8). tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/webview/wizard.html gains a

Choose adoption tier

radio group above

Prerequisites

; default is Full to preserve existing behavior. data-tier="full" / data-tier="lightweight" attributes tag prerequisites, callouts, and buttons; applyTierVisibility(tier) toggles .hidden class on all [data-tier] elements on radio change. New Lightweight prerequisite (path-aware review agent) and no-API-spend callout surface only under Lightweight selection. The Configure AI Router + Show Cost Dashboard buttons hide under Lightweight; Troubleshoot stays for both. pricingLink click handler now guarded with if (pricing) since the link lives inside a hideable callout.

Commit E (fd82944): doc revisions + cross-repo notice. (1) docs/session-state-schema.md § Tier expectations rewritten to describe actual Set 048 model: router writers DO operate under --no-router; lazy LLM-SDK imports keep credentials out; verification short-circuits to manual attestation; external-verification.md soft gate; hand-maintained Lightweight migration path. (2) docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md Step 6 gains 'Lightweight tier — copyable review prompts replace routed verification' subsection: 5-step copy / paste / paste-back / soft-gate flow with the path-aware-agent requirement. Ran docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity/run_s4_verification.py. Route (gpt-5-4, tier 3, $0.175) returned needs-attention with 1 Medium finding: apply-mode backup re-read race in migrate_one_set's _atomic_copy_json (concurrent edit between initial parse and backup re-read would have raised JSONDecodeError out of the migrator, breaking the 'never raises on normal failure' contract). The two partial-pass checks (#2 refusal correctness + #8 spec compliance) shared the same root cause. The verify-of-verify step was attempted but the cross-provider verifier path was unavailable for gpt-5-4; the route() call already used task_type=session-verification cross-provider routing so the route response IS the Round-A verdict. S4 verification cost: $0.175 of $10 NTE; cumulative Set 048 routed spend ~$0.76 (counting an earlier first-pass route that crashed on a RouteResult attribute access bug in the script, the actual paid spend is closer to $0.76, ~7.6% of NTE).

Medium #1 fix addressed in-flight per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope: migrate_one_set apply path now writes the already-parsed `state` dict directly via _atomic_write_json(backup_path, state) instead of re-reading the source file. No re-read, no race. New regression test test_backup_uses_parsed_state_not_reread_from_disk monkey-patches the writer to confirm the backup content reflects the parsed dict, not whatever ends up on disk after concurrent edits. 17 Lightweight-migrator tests pass (was 16); 0 regressions elsewhere.

Items cover §3.1 (three-knob precedence + override logging + free-form-prose tier rejection), §3.1 A3 (route/verify short-circuit + fail-CLOSED), §3.5 (TTY prompt + non-TTY proceed + --accept-suggestions), §3.4 + §3.6 (tri-state schema + tier default), §3.2 (path-reference + git-range + gating + review-criteria embed + slug sanitization), L5 (dual-action non-terminal vs terminal), §3.3 (QuickPick + submenu + L4 close-on-blur + L3 openAiAssignment removal), §3.7 (migrator happy path + idempotence + refusal + backup-race fix), §3.8 (single-set + multi-set picker), §3.9 (templates), E8 wizard radio + visibility + restore, doc-revision spot-checks, Python + TS suite + .vsix + PyPI build artifacts.

Executing the UAT smoke test for route()/verify() short-circuit under DABBLER_NO_ROUTER=1 discovered a Critical bug: `from runtime_mode import is_no_router_mode` in ai_router/__init__.py:317 (route() prologue) raised `ModuleNotFoundError`. Investigation: 5 production-code sites use bare imports of the new Set 048 modules (`from runtime_mode import …`, `from spec_config import …`) — __init__.py:317 (route), :617 (verify), start_session.py:843 (main), close_session.py:1644 (run), runtime_mode.py:75 (_spec_tier). Bare form only resolves under the test conftest.py's sys.path shim; pip-installed consumers (the Lightweight target audience) hit ModuleNotFoundError. route() and verify() exploded outright; start_session.main() and close_session.run() wrapped the bare import in `try/except: pass`, so --no-router was a silent no-op for the entire production CLI surface. The original S2 Round-A verifier flagged this as Major #2 and the finding was dismissed as a false positive on conftest grounds; that dismissal was wrong.

Commit A (58a8f35): all 5 production-code sites switched to relative imports (`from .runtime_mode import …`, `from .spec_config import …`). New test_production_imports.py::test_no_bare_imports_of_set048_modules_in_production_code static-analysis guard. ai_router/tests/conftest.py adds module-aliasing block: sys.modules[bare_name] = sys.modules['ai_router.<mod>'] for the four Set 048 modules with module-level cache state. test_runtime_mode.py caplog filter updated from logger='runtime_mode' to logger='ai_router.runtime_mode' (matches the relative-import-resolved __name__). Smoke test under simulated pip-install sys.path: route() returns RouteResult(cost_usd=0.0, model_name='no-router-mode') after fix.

Ran docs/session-sets/048-lightweight-tier-parity/run_s5_verification.py. Route (gpt-5-4, tier 3, $0.1806) returned pass_with_findings with 3 findings: Major #1 (static-analysis guard narrower than docstring — only top-level *.py + only from-form; misses bare `import` + subpackage + whitespace variants); Minor #2 (conftest alias is import-order sensitive — pre-conftest bare imports could leave alias replacing different module object while existing references stay bound to old one); Minor #3 (UAT checklist covered §3.x + L3-L5 but not explicitly P1 — model/effort/session-set/session ident + state-file updates). Verify-of-verify skipped (no cross-provider verifier configured for gpt-5-4; route() was task_type=session-verification so route response IS Round-A verdict). S5 verification cost: $0.1806 of $10 NTE; cumulative Set 048 spend $0.94 (9.4%).

All 3 Round-A findings addressed in-flight per memory feedback_dont_hide_behind_out_of_scope (Commit C a24ab8a): Major #1 — test_production_imports.py rewritten on ast.walk over ai_router/**/*.py excluding tests/; rejects both ImportFrom (level==0, module in set) and Import (bare alias.name in set); 9 new scanner self-tests cover bare from-import + indented from-import + bare import statement + import-with-alias + relative acceptance + package-absolute acceptance + unrelated-module acceptance + subpackage walk + tests-dir exclusion. Minor #2 — conftest defensive check raises RuntimeError if sys.modules[bare_name] already exists and is a different object than ai_router.<mod>; fail-fast beats silent split-identity bug. Minor #3 — 3 new UAT items added (P1 parity Identification + State-file updates + Events ledger); Feedback fields document the architectural invariant that --no-router short-circuits only route/verify calls, never state-file writers. 1020 Python pass (was 1011; +9 scanner self-tests); 0 regressions.
